hypothesis: Enhancing steric and electrostatic complementarity at positions 5 and 6 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming unique interactions with SSTR2-specific residues in ECL2 and TM5, while avoiding off-target SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are capable of strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity, but structural quality remains a bottleneck. Focusing on structural refinement and diversity expansion is critical.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pass rate indicates insufficient diversity in the candidate pool. Increasing the number of candidates may improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
Structural failures likely stem from poor geometry or clashes. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better packing and reduce structural artifacts.
